eBay First Stream · GMV Conversion Analysis
Jan 2026 – Jun 2026 · Data refresh: 2026-06-09 · Filter by country & category per tab
Success = GMV > $0 on first stream  |  Comparison = GMV = $0 on first stream
Seller Profile — First Stream Conversion

What separates sellers who make a sale on their very first live stream from those who don't? Converter = GMV > $0 on first stream date. Non-converter = GMV = $0. All metrics anchored to the first stream date only — use filters to slice by market and category.

Country
Category
✓ Embedded · 8 countries · 92 categories
The 21-Day Journey — What Happens After Stream 1

Converters re-stream faster, more often, and compound GMV across all three post-stream weeks. Non-converters face a hard 7-day recovery window — after day 7, the probability of return drops from 71.8% to 8.3%. Filter by country and category to see the pattern in your market.

Country
Category
✓ Q10 embedded · 202 rows · 8 countries
Actionable Recommendations — First Stream Conversion & Retention

Derived from signal bucket data (Q4, n=3,361 sellers), re-stream timing (Q8), first-stream signal profiles (Q9), and 21-day post-first-stream trajectory (Q10). Duration and chat are confirmed as dual-ROI levers — they predict both first-stream GMV conversion and re-stream velocity. New from Q10: converter advantage compounds across all three post-first-stream weeks; non-converters who return within 7 days recover meaningfully, but the window is narrow.

ℹ All-market data unless noted. Funnel numbers are based on the full dataset (n=3,361 sellers, all countries, all categories). For country- or category-specific cuts, use the Signal Buckets, Retention, or Trajectory tabs with filters applied.

NEW — Trajectory insight (Q10): Converter advantage is not a first-stream story — it compounds across all three post-stream weeks. Converters re-stream at 75–87% within 7 days (vs. ~50% non-converters) and maintain 90%+ sale stream rates in weeks 2 and 3. Total 21-day GMV for converters runs 3–5× their first-stream GMV (e.g., US Coins: $20,845 avg first stream, $16,632 avg per stream over 21 days across 1,097 streams). Non-converters who return within 7 days do recover — 40–70% sale stream rates in weeks 2–3 — but recovery requires returning within the 7-day window. Re-stream rate drops from 71.8% (days 0–7) to 8.3% (days 8–15): every action on this tab is designed to keep sellers inside that window.
Lever 1 — Activation  ·  Duration, chat, and inventory readiness before and during stream 1
1
Pre-stream coaching + in-stream moderation — duration, chat, and inventory in one motion
Live Ops · Seller Enablement · Seller App  · Consolidates former Actions 1–4
5.3% → 88%
conversion across duration tiers
32.6% → 80.6%
conversion across chat tiers
450 vs 155
avg chat: 0–7 day vs never-return
160 vs 99 min
avg duration: 0–7 day vs never-return
  • 48h before first stream: send a category-specific question bank email (10 opener prompts), a duration goal ("Stream 60+ min — 88% of sellers who did made a sale"), and an inventory warning if the seller has fewer than 40 active listings. Q10 shows converters averaged 187–522 listed items on their first stream across major markets — inventory readiness is a genuine signal.
  • At the 25-minute mark: surface an in-app banner with their live conversion probability gauge and the milestone: "Sellers who stream 60+ min convert at 88% — keep going." At 55 min, a second nudge: "Top sellers stream 2+ hours. Your viewers are still with you."
  • First 30 minutes: assign a Live Ops moderator to every first-time seller's stream. Their job: post 3–5 opener questions from the category question bank to break chat silence and push the seller past the critical 21-chat threshold (where conversion crosses 80%).
  • Chat anomaly — segment before scaling: zero-chat sellers convert at 52.7%, outperforming the 1–20 chat group (32.6%). These are likely established sellers with pre-built audiences — not new-to-live sellers. Identify these sellers via GMV history before applying chat coaching at scale; moderation is most critical for the 310 sellers in the 1–20 chat range.
Live Ops Marketing Seller Experience Engineering  Dual ROI confirmed (Q9 + Q10): longer first streams with more chat → higher same-day conversion AND higher 7-day re-stream probability
Lever 2 — Retention  ·  The 7-day re-stream window determines 21-day GMV for all sellers
2
Post-stream golden hour — instant win push for high-signal sellers, booking modal for all
Seller CRM · Seller App · Post-stream data trigger  · Consolidates former Actions 5–6
89.4%
0–7 day returners converted on stream 1
71.8% → 8.3%
re-stream rate: days 0–7 vs 8–15
570
sellers with no return in 21 days
< 2h
optimal trigger window post-stream
  • For converter + ≥ 50 chat (within 2h of stream end): personalized push — "You made your first live sales — your buyers are still warm. Sellers who stream again within 7 days build lasting momentum. Tap to schedule Stream 2." These sellers had 89.4% first-stream conversion and 450 avg chat messages — highest-ROI cohort to retain.
  • For all sellers (within 5 min of stream end): "Book Stream 2" full-screen modal. Pre-populate: same time, 7 days from now — one tap to confirm. For converters: "Your buyers remember you — don't let 7 days pass." For non-converters: "Most sellers find their rhythm on Stream 2 — here's what to try next time."
  • Trajectory confirms urgency (Q10): converters who re-stream in week 1 maintain 90%+ sale stream rates in weeks 2 and 3 — their GMV compounds rather than fading. Non-converters who return within 7 days generate real subsequent GMV (40–70% sale stream rates in weeks 2–3). Both cohorts gain almost nothing from re-streaming after day 14.
Engineering CRM  Booking modal targets all 3,361 sellers (medium eng). Instant push requires post-stream chat + GMV signal detection — medium effort, very high ROI for the top-performing cohort
3
Day 5 urgency rescue — re-engage at-risk sellers before the 7-day window closes
Seller CRM · Push notification · Email
Day 7
habit window closes
8.3%
return in days 8–15 (vs 71.8% in 0–7)
~600
sellers without a booking by day 5
81% / 71%
0–7 day group still active in weeks 2 / 3
  • At Day 5 post-first-stream, if Stream 2 is not yet scheduled: push + email — "You have 2 days left in your peak window. 72% of live sellers stream again within a week. [Schedule now →]"
  • At Day 7 (final warning), if still unscheduled: last-chance SMS (opted-in sellers) — "Today is your peak re-stream day. Go live for even 30 minutes — your buyers still remember you."
  • A/B test loss-aversion framing ("Don't lose your audience") vs. positive reinforcement ("Your viewers are ready for more"). Primary KPI: re-stream within 7 days. Secondary: 21-day GMV.
CRM Engineering  Target: ~600 sellers not booked by Day 5. Getting 30% back within 7 days ≈ 180 additional early re-streamers. Ships as pure CRM automation — no eng dependency beyond the trigger
4
Non-converter debrief — personalized gap analysis with explicit week-1 re-stream urgency
Seller CRM · Email · Data pipeline  · Updated: trajectory-informed framing
523
non-converting first streams
7 days
target re-stream window
40–70%
nc re-streamer sale rate in weeks 2–3 (Q10)
  • Key message (Q10 trajectory): non-converters who return within 7 days and make changes do generate GMV in weeks 2–3 — 40–70% sale stream rates across categories. The first-stream outcome is not permanent. But every day past day 7 closes that recovery path.
  • Within 2h of stream end: "Here's what happened — and why streaming again this week changes everything." Include exact gaps: their duration vs. 121-min converter median, viewer count vs. 270 median, chat vs. 158 median.
  • For each gap, prescribe a specific fix: duration gap → "Schedule a 2-hour block this week"; viewer gap → "We'll promote your stream to [N] buyers in [category] if you go live within 7 days"; chat gap → "Try these 5 opener questions."
  • Include a one-click "Schedule Stream 2 — This Week" button. Remove all friction from the re-attempt decision.
Engineering CRM  Addresses 523 non-converters. Framed as "your GMV trajectory is still open" — not "try again." Requires per-seller gap computation from Q5/Q10 data pipeline
5
NEW — 21-day momentum program: week 2 and week 3 CRM touches for active re-streamers
Seller CRM · Email / Push  · New — derived from Q10 trajectory analysis
90%+
converter sale stream rate in weeks 2 & 3
3–5×
total 21-day GMV vs first-stream GMV (converters)
Days 8–21
untouched CRM window today
  • New insight from Q10: converters who re-stream in week 1 don't just sustain — they compound. Week 2 and week 3 sale stream rates hold above 90% across major markets. Total 21-day GMV runs 3–5× first-stream GMV (US Coins: avg $20,845 first stream → $17.8M across 1,097 streams over 21 days; US Collectibles: $9,250 → $4.4M over 1,768 streams). This compounding only happens if sellers stay in the habit loop through week 3.
  • Day 10 (start of week 2): push to any seller who streamed in week 1 — "You're building momentum. Week 2 sellers like you generate [X]× their first-stream GMV on average. Your buyers are watching."
  • Day 17 (start of week 3): push to sellers who streamed in both weeks 1 and 2 — "You're in the top [X]% of live sellers who reach week 3. Sellers who complete 3 weeks lock in their habit — and their GMV compounds from here."
  • For non-converters who re-streamed: reinforce improvement — "Your sale rate improved from week 1. Here's your next milestone." Ground this in their actual Q10 trajectory data.
CRM  New action from Q10. Zero additional engineering — pure CRM automation on the stream activity feed. High ROI: keeps sellers in a compounding GMV loop through the full 21-day window
Lever 3 — Acquisition  ·  Viewer volume is the single biggest first-stream conversion unlock
6
Category browse boost + buyer push notification at go-live for first-time sellers
Marketplace · Discovery algorithm · Engineering
5.8%
1–10 viewers conversion
47.6%
11–50 viewers conversion
80.6%
51–200 viewers conversion
95.0%
200+ viewers conversion
  • Getting a new seller from 1–10 viewers to 51–200 jumps conversion from 5.8% → 80.6%. This is the single highest-leverage variable in the dataset — one tier jump from near-zero to category-median viewer count eliminates most of the first-stream conversion gap.
  • Automatically surface first-time seller streams in the top 3 slots of the relevant category browse page for the first 60 minutes. Label them "New Seller Live."
  • Trigger a push notification to buyers who purchased from similar sellers in the last 90 days. Verify eBay-follower push notifications are firing at stream start for all new sellers and check opt-in rates — this may already exist but not be consistently activated.
  • 69 sellers are currently stuck at 1–10 viewers (5.8% conversion). Moving them to the 51–200 tier would add ~52 additional first-stream GMV events — before any retention improvement.
Engineering Marketplace  High complexity — requires algorithmic change to category browse ranking. Highest single-variable uplift potential in the data. Ship after retention loop (Actions 2–5) is in place

This sprint (no eng): Actions 3, 4, 5 — pure CRM and email content. Pre-stream question bank email (part of Action 1) also ships here. Next week (ops-led pilot): Action 1 moderator seeding — no engineering required for manual pilot. Medium eng (paired): Actions 1 (in-app nudges) and 2 (post-stream push + booking modal) — ship as the habit-loop feature set. Next quarter: Action 6 (category browse boost) — highest leverage, requires algorithmic work.

Methodology — First Stream GMV Conversion

This dashboard answers a single question: what attributes of a seller's first live stream predict whether they will make a sale that day? The companion dashboard (First-Stream-Analysis_20260615.html) uses re-stream timing as the success metric; this one uses same-day GMV outcome.

Success definition

GroupConditionField
SuccessGMV > $0 on first stream datemade_sale_flag = 1
Not successGMV = $0 on first stream datemade_sale_flag = 0
First streamEarliest cal_dt with event_duration_min > 0 and deleted_ind = 0ROW_NUMBER() rank = 1
Cohort start2026-01-01 onwardscal_dt >= '2026-01-01'

Source tables

TableUsed for
P_AMER_VERTICALS_T.LIVE_COMMERCE_DAILY_DEMAND_INDICATORSGMV, sold items, viewers, buyers, bidders, duration, country (slr_cntry_cd), category (l1_categ_nm)
P_AMER_VERTICALS_T.LIVE_COMMERCE_DAILY_DEMAND_INDICATORS_DEDUPEChat count, like count, unique viewer count

Query 3 / Query 5 — Converter vs Non-Converter by Cohort Week

Groups sellers by DATE_TRUNC('week', first_stream_date) and computes avg + median for every engagement metric, separately for made_sale_flag = 1 and made_sale_flag = 0. Query 5 is Query 3 extended with slr_cntry_cd and l1_categ_nm GROUP BY columns. Country and category breakdowns are embedded — use filter bars to slice directly.

Query 4 / Query 6 — Signal Bucket Analysis

Divides sellers into buckets by each engagement signal and computes pct_made_sale per bucket. Query 6 is Query 4 extended with country and category dimensions. Signal bucket data is embedded with country and category dimensions.

SignalBuckets
viewer_count1–10  |  11–50  |  51–200  |  200+
stream_duration_min<10 min  |  10–30 min  |  31–60 min  |  61–120 min  |  120 min+
chat_count0  |  1–20  |  21–100  |  101–500  |  500+
like_count0  |  1–50  |  51–200  |  201–1,000  |  1,000+

Query 7 — Items Sold on First Stream

Extends Q5 by adding si_during (sold-item count) from LIVE_COMMERCE_DAILY_DEMAND_INDICATORS. Two new output columns: cv_avg_items_sold and cv_med_items_sold (converter side only — non-converters by definition sold 0 items). Once Q7 CSV is embedded, the Overview and Metric Comparison tabs gain an "Items sold" row.

Query 8 — Re-stream Timing Buckets (21-day pattern)

For every seller who had a first stream in 2026, identifies when (if ever) they streamed again within 21 days. Sellers are bucketed by days-to-first-restream:

BucketMeaning
0–7 daysRe-streamed within the first week
8–15 daysRe-streamed in days 8–15
16–21 daysRe-streamed in days 16–21
21+ / neverDid not re-stream within 21 days (includes late re-streamers and those who never returned)

For each bucket, the 21-day window is divided into three sub-windows (days 1–7, 8–15, 16–21) showing active sellers, stream count, total GMV, and % streams with a sale. Output: country × category × restream_bucket. The Re-stream Pattern section appears at the bottom of all four filter tabs, filtered by each tab's country/category selection.

How country & category filters work

  1. Data is already embedded — Q5 (954 rows, 8 countries, 92 categories) and Q6 (1,393 rows) are baked into this file. No upload required.
  2. Select a country — the category dropdown automatically narrows to categories available in that country. When "All" countries is selected, the category dropdown shows the top 12 categories by seller volume across all markets.
  3. Filters are per-tab and independent — Overview, Metric Comparison, Signal Buckets, and By Week each have their own Country + Category selection.
  4. Weighted averages are used when re-aggregating across multiple cohort weeks after filtering. Medians shown are weighted averages of per-group medians (approximation).
  5. % Repeat Streamer (By Week and Signal Buckets tabs) is only available in the unfiltered All / All view — this field comes from Query 3 / Query 4 and is not in the Q5 / Q6 segmented data. Filtered views show "—" for that column.
  6. Actions tab is always all-market (n=3,361). Its numbers do not change with filter selections — see Signal Buckets with a filter applied for country- or category-specific conversion rates.
